perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored) , including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed un der this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may r equest it to the Receiving Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored) , including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed un der this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may r equest it to the Receiving Party .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the option for the Receiving Party to 'destroy' all copies. This creates ambiguity in clause 3.8 where it says 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law'. If destruction is no longer an option, the 'retain such of its documents' clause becomes contradictory, since it is uncertain under what circumstances the receiving party would be able to keep copies of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.8 Notw ithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Information or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the option for the Receiving Party to destroy copies of Confidential Information, which directly contradicts clause 3.8. Clause 3.8 states 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law'. By removing the option to destroy, it creates a situation where the conditions under which the Receiving Party would be able to keep copies of the confidential information are no longer clear, establishing a strong in-text contradiction as described in the provided explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shal l be co n- strued as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a licence expressly or by implication under any patent, copyrigh t or other intelle c- tual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all exis t- ing and future intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Inf ormation are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of cla rity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for or obtain any intellectual proper ty protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the R eceiving Party shall be the sole prope r- ty of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shal l be co n- strued as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a licence expressly or by implication under any patent, copyrigh t or other intelle c- tual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all exis t- ing and future intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Inf ormation are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of cla rity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for intellectual proper ty protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the R eceiving Party shall be the sole prope r- ty of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text specifically says the Receiving Party will not 'apply for or obtain' any intellectual property. The modified text removes 'or obtain'. This contradicts the last sentence 'Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Party shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Party'. It is unclear whether the Receiving party obtaining Intellectual Property through means other than applying it is the Disclosing Party's property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shal l be co n- strued as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a licence expressly or by implication under any patent, copyrigh t or other intelle c- tual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all exis t- ing and future intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Inf ormation are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of cla rity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for intellectual proper ty protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the R eceiving Party shall be the sole prope r- ty of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party will not 'apply for or obtain' intellectual property protection. The changed text removes 'or obtain', stating the Receiving Party will not 'apply for' intellectual property protection. This creates a contradiction with the clause 'Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Party shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Party' because it is now unclear if the Receiving party obtaining Intellectual Property through means other than applying for it (for instance creating it independently) is the Disclosing Party's property, which breaks compatibility and is a strong contradiction. The Contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but that is because the change happens within the same section as the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.2 If the Parties succeed in the call for proposal referred to in clause 2 and sign the corresponding Grant Agreement (GA) and Consortium Agreement (CA), or enter into partnership under any other kind of collaborative agreement (COA) or association agreement (AA) , the non -disclosure provisions of the CA, COA and AA shall [be suppl e- mented by or supplement or supersede ] this Agreement. In the event that non -disclosure provisions are not provided for the said private agreements , this Agreement shall remain in force until the end of the collaboration undertaken or after [months or years ] of its termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.2 If the Parties succeed in the call for proposal referred to in clause 2 and sign the corresponding Grant Agreement (GA) and Consortium Agreement (CA), or enter into partnership under any other kind of collaborative agreement (COA) or association agreement (AA) , the non -disclosure provisions of the CA, COA and AA shall be supplemented by this Agreement. In the event that non -disclosure provisions are not provided for the said private agreements , this Agreement shall remain in force until the end of the collaboration undertaken or after [months or years ] of its termination.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by forcing the agreement to be 'supplemented by' the other agreements. The original text allows it to be 'supplemented by or supplement or supersede'. This contradicts with the phrase 'this Agreement shall remain in force until the end of the collaboration' since the new text implies that the NDAs in the subsequent agreements could not supersede this agreement at any point.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.2 If the Parties succeed in the call for proposal referred to in clause 2 and sign the corresponding Grant Agreement (GA) and Consortium Agreement (CA), or enter into partnership under any other kind of collaborative agreement (COA) or association agreement (AA) , the non -disclosure provisions of the CA, COA and AA shall be supplemented by this Agreement. In the event that non -disclosure provisions are not provided for the said private agreements , this Agreement shall remain in force until the end of the collaboration undertaken or after [months or years ] of its termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the possibility for subsequent agreements to supersede the current agreement (by removing '[be supplemented by or supplement or supersede]'). This creates an in-text contradiction with the latter half of the paragraph which states 'this Agreement shall remain in force', since the removed text would have otherwise allowed it to be superseded by other NDAs.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information r e- ceived, except to its employees, representatives or ag ents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the informati on provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the oblig a- tions of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information r e- ceived, except to its employees who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the informati on provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the oblig a- tions of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the disclosure of confidential information to only 'employees', removing 'representatives or agents'. This contradicts the end of the section, 'will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel', which now implies only employees and no longer agents or representatives. It creates uncertainty in who the Receiving party is responsible for under this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information r e- ceived, except to its employees who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the informati on provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the oblig a- tions of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 3.2 by removing 'representatives or agents' from those to whom confidential information can be disclosed. The original text allows disclosure to 'employees, representatives or agents', whereas the changed text only allows disclosure to 'employees'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case. The change therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party is only liable for acts or omissions of 'personnel', which now implies only 'employees', and creates uncertainty regarding liability for 'representatives or agents'. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
